definitions.definition: A value that can be calculated from a square matrix and which can be used to determine the solvability and nature of the solutions of a system of linear equations.
definitions.example: For a 2x2 matrix, the determinant is calculated as ad - bc.

definitions.definition: A matrix with the same number of rows and columns.
definitions.example: Example of a 3x3 square matrix: [[a, b, c], [d, e, f], [g, h, i]].

definitions.definition: The determinant of the matrix obtained by removing the row and column of the element for which the minor is being calculated.
definitions.example: Minor of element a is calculated as the determinant of the 2x2 matrix [[e, f], [h, i]].

definitions.definition: The minor of an element multiplied by -1 raised to the power of the sum of its row and column indices.
definitions.example: Cofactor of element a is calculated as (-1)^(1+1) * minor of a.

definitions.definition: The product of the elements on the main diagonal minus the product of the elements on the other diagonal.
definitions.example: Determinant of a 2x2 matrix [[a, b], [c, d]] is ad - bc.

definitions.definition: The sum of the products of each element in a row (or column) with its cofactor.
definitions.example: Determinant of a 3x3 matrix [[a, b, c], [d, e, f], [g, h, i]] is a(ei - fh) - b(di - fg) + c(dh - eg).

definitions.definition: A general method of calculating the determinant for matrices of any size.
definitions.example: The determinant of a matrix can be calculated using the Laplace expansion method.

definitions.definition: A method of calculating the determinant of a matrix by expanding along a row or column.
definitions.example: The Laplace expansion method involves multiplying each element in a row or column by its cofactor and summing the results.

definitions.definition: A matrix that, when multiplied by the original matrix, gives the identity matrix.
definitions.example: The inverse of a matrix A is denoted as A^(-1) and satisfies the equation AA^(-1) = I.

definitions.definition: The maximum number of linearly independent rows or columns in a matrix.
definitions.example: The rank of a matrix can be found by calculating its determinant or by performing row operations.

definitions.definition: Vectors that cannot be expressed as a linear combination of each other.
definitions.example: The vectors [1, 0] and [0, 1] are linearly independent.

definitions.definition: Vectors that can be expressed as a linear combination of each other.
definitions.example: The vectors [1, 0] and [2, 0] are linearly dependent.

definitions.definition: A method of solving a system of linear equations using determinants.
definitions.example: Cramer's Rule involves replacing each column of the coefficient matrix with the constant matrix and calculating the determinants.

definitions.definition: A set of linear equations involving multiple variables.
definitions.example: The system of linear equations x + y = 2 and x - y = 0 has multiple solutions.

definitions.definition: A system of linear equations where all the constant terms are zero.
definitions.example: The homogeneous system of linear equations x + y = 0 and x - y = 0 has only the trivial solution.

definitions.definition: A solution to a system of linear equations where all the variables are equal to zero.
definitions.example: The trivial solution to the system of linear equations x + y = 0 and x - y = 0 is x = 0 and y = 0.

definitions.definition: A solution to a system of linear equations where not all the variables are equal to zero.
definitions.example: The system of linear equations x + y = 2 and x - y = 0 has a non-trivial solution.

definitions.definition: The matrix obtained by transposing the cofactor matrix.
definitions.example: The adjugate matrix of a 3x3 matrix [[a, b, c], [d, e, f], [g, h, i]] is the transpose of the cofactor matrix.
